title: Room Choices — Branching Paths
description: Add decision-making to the game. After displaying the starting room, let the player choose a direction (north, south, east). Use if/else statements to describe what happens in each direction. Add a switch statement to handle actions in the new room (1=Explore, 2=Rest, 3=Go back). Each choice should have a different outcome (e.g., finding gold, losing health, discovering an item). Include at least 3 case labels in your switch. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'if' in cpp, 'Use if statements to handle direction choices'
assert 'else' in cpp, 'Use else to handle alternative directions'
assert 'switch' in cpp, 'Use a switch statement for room actions'
assert cpp.count('case') >= 3, 'Include at least 3 case labels in the switch statement'
import re
if_else_count = len(re.findall(r'\bif\b', cpp)) + len(re.findall(r'\belse\b', cpp))
assert if_else_count >= 3, 'Use at least 3 if/else branches for direction handling'
acceptance_criteria: if/else statements handle each direction with unique room descriptions
A switch statement handles at least 3 actions in the new room
Choices affect player stats (health, gold, etc.)
Updated stats are displayed after choices
hints: Use switch(action) with case 1:, case 2:, case 3: and don't forget break;
Exploring might add gold (gold += 10), resting might restore health (health += 20)
Add a default: case to handle invalid input
